'''Tony Isabella''' (born [[December 22]], [[1950s|1951]]) is an [[Wikipedia:United States|American]] comic book writer and commentator, who wrote the comic "[[Routine]]." He is best known as the creator and writer of [[Marvel Comics]]' [[Wikipedia:Black Goliath|Black Goliath]] and [[Wikipedia:Black Lightning|Black Lightning]], DC Comics' first major African American superhero.

==Biography==
He began as an editorial assistant at [[Marvel Comics]] in 1972. At Marvel, he wrote [[Wikipedia:Ghost Rider (comics)|''Ghost Rider'']], [[Wikipedia:It, the Living Colossus|It, the Living Colossus]] in [[Wikipedia:Astonishing Tales|''Astonishing Tales'']], [[Wikipedia:Luke Cage|Luke Cage]] in [[Wikipedia:Hero for Hire|''Hero for Hire'']] and [[Wikipedia:Power Man|''Power Man'']], Tigra in ''Marvel Chillers'', [[Wikipedia:The Champions (comics)|''The Champions'']], and [[Wikipedia:Captain America|''Captain America'']]. He also served as editor of Marvel's black and white magazine line for a time. Before he joined Marvel, Mr. Isabella had a letter to the editor published in Tales of Suspense #92, positively reviewing the Iron Man tale in Tales of Suspense #89.

For DC Comics, he worked as a writer and story editor. In addition to two runs on ''Black Lightning'' (one in the 1970s, the other in the 1990s), in the 1980s he revamped [[Wikipedia:Hawkman|Hawkman]] and [[Wikipedia:Hawkwoman|Hawkwoman]] with artist Richard Howell in the mini-series ''Shadow War of Hawkman''. The mini-series led to a regular series which he initially wrote.

He is the co-author of the short story ''If Wishes Were Horses...'' and the novels ''Captain America: Liberty's Torch'' and ''[[Wikipedia:Star Trek|Star Trek]]: The Case Of The Colonist's Corpse (A Sam Cogley Mystery)''.

He writes a regular column for the [[Wikipedia:Comics Buyer's Guide|''Comics Buyer's Guide'']], called ''Tony's Tips''. This has resulted in a regular (and, given the different demands of the medium, much more frequent) online column/blog called ''Tony's Online Tips''.

He has also recently worked on translating foreign language Disney comics and revamping the wording for the US such as "Walt Disney's Uncle Scrooge #354". Also, he has recently announced on his column that he plans on doing new comic book work. He has said that he will work on comics for Heroic Publishing, probably working on that company's superheroine [[Wikipedia:Tigress (Heroic comics)|Tigress]] (not to be confused with the Marvel superheroine Tigra mentioned above or the DC characters).
